title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Transfer Build
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers streaming analysis, chunk boundaries, and resource budgets into a changed bounded streaming analyzer. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded streaming analyzer with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Stream Iteration
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 1299 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-j74m22
# Competency: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence.
# Case 9801: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_batch_stream_equivalence_j74m22(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_j74m22 = "j74m22-22m47j-9801"
    assert evidence_variant_j74m22
    evidence_axes_j74m22 = (
        "corpusj74m22",
        "policy22m47j",
        "revision9801",
        "commandj74m229801",
        "report22m47j9801",
        "test9801j74m22",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_j74m22)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"
